name: Miami-Dade County Public Schools
districtFact: Fourth largest school district in the United States, serving one of the most linguistically and culturally diverse student bodies in the country.

about.title: About the District
about.content: Miami-Dade County Public Schools (M-DCPS) is the fourth largest school district in the United States, serving approximately 330,000 students across more than 550 schools in Miami-Dade County, Florida.

planningTips: In 2026-2027, Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach all share Spring Break March 22–26 — but this tri-county alignment doesn't happen every year. Always verify each county's official calendar before booking travel.

Hurricane season (Aug–Oct) overlaps with the school year. Florida law allows districts to use banked makeup days, but extended closures may push the school year into mid-June. Book late-May travel with flexible cancellation policies.

Miami Beach and Key West hotel prices surge dramatically during Miami-Dade Spring Break week. Book early or consider the week before (when Broward is on break but Miami-Dade is still in school) for lower pricing.
The M-DCPS official calendar is published in English, Spanish, and Haitian Creole — download the version in your family's language from the dadeschools.net website for the most accessible reference.

districtFaqs.q: Is Miami-Dade County Public Schools the same as the University of Miami (UM) or Miami Dade College?
districtFaqs.a: No. Miami-Dade County Public Schools (M-DCPS) is the K-12 public school system serving Miami-Dade County. The University of Miami (UM) is a private research university in Coral Gables, and Miami Dade College (MDC) is a separate public college system. Neither institution shares an academic calendar with M-DCPS. UM and MDC set their own semester schedules independently of the K-12 school district.

districtFaqs.q: Does Miami-Dade have makeup days for hurricanes?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Florida law allows school districts to bank up to three emergency makeup days into the school calendar. Miami-Dade builds these into its schedule to absorb minor weather disruptions. If closures exceed the banked days due to a major hurricane, the district may be required to add makeup days at the end of the school year, potentially extending into mid-June. Families booking late-May or early-June travel should use flexible booking options if a storm has caused significant school closures during the year.

districtFaqs.q: Why does Miami-Dade end the school year later than most Florida districts?
districtFaqs.a: Miami-Dade County Public Schools ends the 2026-2027 school year on June 3 — later than many Florida and national peers. This reflects the district's teacher planning day schedule, its instructional hour requirements, and the calendar structure set by the elected school board. The later end date also provides a buffer for potential hurricane makeup days. Families planning summer activities should keep June 3 in mind as the last student day.

livingHere.intro: Miami-Dade County is Florida's southeastern anchor — a densely international city shaped by Latin American, Caribbean, and global influences. For families relocating here, the city's infrastructure, cultural calendar, and geography each intersect with how school-year life unfolds.

highlights.label: Miami International Airport (MIA)
highlights.detail: One of the top US airports for international passenger volume, with direct service to over 100 Latin American and Caribbean destinations. Flights during Spring Break and the week school ends in early June sell out weeks ahead, especially routes to South America and the Caribbean.

highlights.label: PortMiami
highlights.detail: The world's busiest cruise port, located on Biscayne Bay in downtown Miami. Post-school-year sailings in late May and early June are among the fastest-booked departures — families planning a cruise departure close to the June 3 last day should reserve cabins by February.

highlights.label: Latin Cultural Calendar
highlights.detail: Calle Ocho Festival in March, Carnaval Miami, and a dense schedule of Latin cultural events shape the community calendar year-round. Many M-DCPS families have extended family in Cuba, Colombia, Venezuela, or Haiti — international travel patterns differ from the typical US school district.

highlights.label: Art Basel Miami Beach
highlights.detail: Held in early December, Art Basel transforms Miami Beach into a global art destination and brings heavy traffic, hotel surges, and road congestion across Miami-Dade. It falls during the school year — plan for downtown and beach-area commutes to be significantly slower that week.

highlights.label: Hurricane Season
highlights.detail: Miami-Dade sits in the most hurricane-exposed corridor in the continental US. Peak risk is August–October, coinciding directly with the school year's start. M-DCPS banks makeup days by law, but a major storm can push the school year end past the June 3 target — use refundable bookings for late-May travel.

highlights.label: Biscayne Bay and the Keys
highlights.detail: Biscayne National Park and Everglades National Park are within an hour of most schools. Many Miami families take advantage of spring break or post-school weekends for Keys trips — the Overseas Highway to Key West is heavily congested during Spring Break week.
